name: evidence-synthesis
description: Guidance for structuring claims, evidence, and limitations from retrieved sources.
instruction: Convert retrieved material into claim-evidence-limitation form. Keep disagreements visible and distinguish direct evidence from inference.
guidelines: For each important claim, retain the supporting paper or result and any important qualification.
Explicitly surface methodological limits, benchmark gaps, or weak external validity.
When evidence conflicts, state the disagreement instead of averaging it away.
